name: translate_to_structured_query
description: Translate a natural-language invoice search question into a strict StructuredQuery.

filters.description: Implicit-AND list of filter clauses. No OR, no NOT, no nested groups.

value.description: Scalar / list / [start, end] tuple. Validator enforces per-field type compatibility downstream.

sort.description: Optional [field, direction] — only sortable fields are allowed.

untranslated_intent.description: Verbatim leftover phrasing the LLM couldn't translate. Surfaces as an amber notice in the UI. Never silently drop intent.

aggregate.description: Optional aggregation directive. Set this when the user asks for counts, totals, averages, top-N rankings, or per-vendor / per-status / per-currency breakdowns (e.g. 'how many invoices', 'total spend by vendor', 'top 5 vendors by spend', 'average invoice amount'). Leave null for normal row-level filter queries. When set, the result shape switches from invoice rows to aggregate rows.

op.description: count = rows matching filters. sum/avg require `field`.

field.description: Required for sum/avg, must be null for count.

group_by.description: Optional GROUP BY column. When set, the result is a list of {group, value} rows sorted by value desc, capped at `limit`. Use for phrases like 'per vendor', 'by status', 'top 5 vendors'.
